faa37fb7-388a-4feb-bb05-0abaf929c148.text:
## STANDARD DEDUCTIONS

### Basic Allowance (Minstefradrag)
- 46% of income, capped at NOK 104,450 (2024)
- Minimum NOK 31,800

### Personal Allowance (Personfradrag)
- NOK 88,250 (standard for single taxpayers)

### Interest Deduction
- Interest paid on loans (mortgage, student loans, etc.) is deductible at 22%.

### Union Fee Deduction
- Up to NOK 7,700 per year in union membership fees is deductible.

### Home Office Deduction
- Standard home office deduction: NOK 2,040 per month if you work from home full-time.

b1766a84-5f14-4c24-9be3-195fb4bab7c8.text:
## KEY CONTACTS AND WEBSITES

- UDI: www.udi.no
- UNE (Appeals Board): www.une.no
- IMDI (Integration): www.imdi.no
- Police (registration): www.politiet.no
- Application portal: www.udi.no/soknad

f4161847-8641-4b2c-948a-c4297d1776ac.text:
## EEA/EFTA CITIZENS

- EU/EEA/EFTA citizens have the right to enter, live, and work in Norway without
  a visa or residence permit for stays under 3 months.
- For stays longer than 3 months, they must register at the local police station.
- EU/EEA/EFTA family members of Norwegian citizens may also use these rights.

21575d14-7485-4cd0-9287-2a721a173a2d.text:
## INTEGRATION PROGRAM (INTRODUKSJONSPROGRAMMET)

- Mandatory 2-year (extendable to 3 years) integration program for newly arrived refugees
  and their family members.
- Includes full-time Norwegian language training and social science courses.
- Participants receive a weekly training grant (introduksjonsstønad) during the program.

46b29ab1-2700-450c-9349-141582f4803e.text:
## PARENTAL LEAVE (FORELDREPENGER / SVANGERSKAPSPENGER)

- Total parental leave: 49 weeks at 100% pay OR 59 weeks at 80% pay.
- Mother's quota: 15 weeks (minimum); Father's/second parent quota: 15 weeks.
- Remaining weeks are shared.
- Both parents must have worked for 6 of the last 10 months before birth.

242b2064-dffc-44d3-8b8f-7bc70b324c57.text:
## HOW TO CONTACT / USE NAV

- Website: nav.no
- Personal service: Ditt NAV (My NAV) – log in with BankID or MinID.
- Local NAV office: every municipality has a NAV office for in-person help.
- Phone: 55 55 33 33

1d1faab9-6eeb-4443-8b28-8ffbc4b5b476.text:
## OLD-AGE PENSION (ALDERSPENSJON)

- Flexible withdrawal from age 62 (if you have earned enough pension rights).
- Full state pension available from age 67.
- Pension depends on lifetime employment income; maximum is approximately 18.1% of
  annual income up to 7.1G per earning year.

12443507-052c-4186-bea5-626dcb0b4d17.text:
## INCOME TAX RATES (2024)

### Personal Tax Rates
- General income (alminnelig inntekt): 22% flat rate
- Low-income bracket: 0% (below NOK 70,000 / year)
- Bracket tax (trinnskatt) is applied progressively on top of general income tax:
  - Step 1: 1.7%  on income above NOK 208,050
  - Step 2: 4.0%  on income above NOK 292,850
  - Step 3: 13.6% on income above NOK 670,000
  - Step 4: 16.6% on income above NOK 937,900
  - Step 5: 17.6% on income above NOK 1,350,000

### National Insurance Contributions
- Employees pay 7.9% of gross income to national insurance (trygdeavgift).
- Self-employed pay 11.5%.

ee2ee0ec-7a6c-4d73-a6c4-43842369d3af.text:
# NAV – Norwegian Labour and Welfare Administration – Domain Grounding Data
# Source: NAV (www.nav.no) – for educational/demo use only

## OVERVIEW
NAV (Arbeids- og velferdsetaten) is the Norwegian Labour and Welfare Administration.
It manages approximately one-third of Norway's national budget through schemes such as
unemployment benefits, parental leave, sick pay, disability benefit, and pensions.

7aa0bee0-7cf5-4bab-9534-d5d0d4efa72b.text:
## SICK PAY (SYKEPENGER)

### Employee Entitlement
- 100% of your salary from day 1 of sick leave, paid by your employer for the first
  16 calendar days (arbeidsgiverperioden).
- From day 17, NAV pays sykepenger up to 6G per year (≈ NOK 711,720 in 2024).
- Maximum duration: 52 weeks within a 3-year period.

### Self-Employed Sick Pay
- Self-employed persons are entitled to sykepenger from day 17 at 80% of their income
  (up to 6G), unless they have purchased additional insurance.

### Sick Note (Sykemelding)
- Medical certificate from a doctor is required if illness lasts more than 3 days
  (employees may use self-certification – egenmelding – for up to 3 days, or up
  to 24 days if the employer has joined the IA (Inclusive Workplace) scheme).

ebda5ce9-ca6a-4c08-a24a-78dd867981ed.text:
## D-NUMBER (D-NUMMER)

- A temporary identity number issued to foreign nationals who do not qualify for a
  Norwegian national identity number (fødselsnummer).
- Required for banking, tax registration, and many public services.
- Apply at a tax office (Skatteetaten) or at certain police stations.

de59cba3-8e2b-419a-803b-c289ee991807.text:
## PERMANENT RESIDENCE PERMIT

- Non-EEA citizens may apply after 3 years of continuous legal residence with a work
  or study permit (some permits require 5 years).
- Must pass a Norwegian language test or demonstrate sufficient Norwegian skills.
- Must not have been convicted of serious criminal offences.
- Must be self-sufficient (not dependent on social assistance).

8f241ee7-fcb8-47b2-b5fe-035eac0d8c67.text:
## ASYLUM

- Anyone who fears persecution in their home country can apply for asylum at the Norwegian
  border or at a police station inside Norway.
- UDI processes the application; the interview (asylintervju) is usually conducted within
  a few weeks of arrival.
- If granted protection, refugees receive a residence or settlement permit.
- Rejected asylum seekers can appeal to UNE (Utlendingsnemnda – Immigration Appeals Board).

f3dfee88-37da-4d18-918c-6af1791df34f.text:
## TAX CARD (SKATTEKORT)

- A tax card tells your employer how much to withhold from your salary.
- Retrieve your tax card at skatteetaten.no or via the Skatteetaten app.
- Main card (Trekkpliktig A-inntekt) – used for primary employment.
- Secondary card (Biinntektskort) – used for second jobs; flat 50% rate.

25f42849-01d6-4684-b334-f0322d6428bd.text:
## DISABILITY BENEFIT (UFØRETRYGD)

### Eligibility
- Reduced work capacity by at least 50% due to illness or injury.
- Must have exhausted sick leave and work assessment allowance (AAP) first.

### Amount
- Approximately 66% of previous income (up to 6G).
- Disability benefit is taxable income.

5db0a036-6800-473e-a416-b41052eda820.text:
## COMMON NAV QUESTIONS

Q: How do I register as a job-seeker?
A: Log in at nav.no with BankID or MinID, go to 'Ditt NAV', and complete the job-seeker
   registration form. You must do this before applying for dagpenger.

Q: I just lost my job. What should I do first?
A: Register as a job-seeker at nav.no immediately, then submit a dagpenger (unemployment
   benefit) application. Deadline: you can apply up to 14 days before your last working day.

Q: As a new immigrant, am I entitled to NAV benefits?
A: You are generally entitled to benefits if you have a valid residence permit, a Norwegian
   D-number or national identity number, have been registered as a resident, and meet the
   income or contribution requirements specific to each benefit.

Q: What is meldekort?
A: Every two weeks you must submit a meldekort (activity/status report) confirming you are
   actively seeking work and available for employment. Failure to submit on time can suspend
   your payments.

74c1bced-2c2d-4381-9297-841a8931d778.text:
# Norwegian Tax (Skatteetaten) – Domain Grounding Data
# Source: Skatteetaten (www.skatteetaten.no) – for educational/demo use only

## OVERVIEW
The Norwegian Tax Administration (Skatteetaten) is responsible for collecting taxes
and for the National Population Register in Norway.

b392cefd-8874-4a0c-9c13-633c30c59f5c.text:
## COMMON IMMIGRATION QUESTIONS

Q: I am from outside the EU/EEA. Can I work in Norway?
A: Yes, but you first need a work permit (tillatelse til å jobbe). You usually apply and
   receive a decision before you travel to Norway. You need a concrete job offer that
   meets Norwegian wage and working-condition standards.

Q: How long does it take to process a work permit?
A: Processing times vary: typically 2–8 weeks for skilled worker permits when applications
   are complete and submitted online.

Q: I just arrived as a refugee. What are my rights?
A: You have the right to accommodation in an asylum reception centre (asylmottak) while
   your case is processed. You may be allowed to work while waiting if processing takes
   more than 6 months. If granted protection, you enter the integration program.

Q: What documents do I need for a residence permit as a family member?
A: Passport, proof of relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate), documentation
   that the reference person in Norway meets the income requirements, and completed
   application forms from udi.no.

Q: I have lived in Norway for 3 years on a work permit. Can I apply for permanent residency?
A: Generally yes, provided you have held a continuous work/residence permit, meet the
   income requirements, have not broken conditions of your permit, and can demonstrate
   sufficient Norwegian or Sami language skills.

79123aa7-fe8d-4149-9924-7e0f485b1169.text:
## UNEMPLOYMENT BENEFITS (DAGPENGER)

### Eligibility
- You must be registered as a job-seeker at NAV before receiving dagpenger.
- You must have earned at least NOK 177,394 in the last 12 months, OR NOK 354,788 in
  the last 36 months (2024 figures).
- You must be a genuine job-seeker and available for work.

### Amount
- 62.4% of your average income over the last 12 or 36 months, up to a maximum of
  6 × the National Insurance Basic Amount (Grunnbeløp, G).
- Maximum daily rate: approximately NOK 778 per day (2024).

### Duration
- Up to 104 weeks (approximately 2 years) if you earned more than 2G in the preceding year.
- Up to 52 weeks if you earned between 0.5G and 2G.

### How to Apply
1. Register as a job-seeker at nav.no.
2. Submit the dagpenger application online (søknad om dagpenger) at nav.no.
3. Send bi-weekly activity reports (meldekort).
4. Payment is made every two weeks.

3580d74b-a2d6-4fa7-8c1b-b8533539fe2f.text:
## VALUE ADDED TAX (MVA / MOMS)

- Standard VAT rate: 25%
- Reduced rate (food and certain goods): 15%
- Low rate (passenger transport, hotel, cinema): 12%
- Exempt: health services, financial services, education

5779a8c1-c754-4004-ba1e-3c823ee70fa2.text:
## WEALTH TAX (FORMUESSKATT)

- Applied nationally (0.3%) and municipally (0.7%) on net wealth above NOK 1,700,000.
- Primary home valued at 25% of market value; secondary home at 100%.

927f4edb-cbf8-43ef-af03-7b302e7919c7.text:
## VISA

### Schengen Visa
- Citizens of many countries outside the EEA/EU require a Schengen visa for short stays
  (up to 90 days in 180 days) in Norway.
- Apply at the Norwegian embassy or consulate in your home country.
- Processing time: minimum 15 calendar days; often 2–6 weeks.
- Fees: approximately EUR 80 (adults).

2983e47b-4176-4cb2-a99a-05b540e24e34.text:
## HOW TO FILE A TAX RETURN

1. The tax return is pre-filled (forhåndsutfylt skattemelding) every April.
2. Log in at skatteetaten.no using BankID or MinID.
3. Review all entries carefully – add any missing income or deductions.
4. Submit by 30 April (for employees) or 31 May (for self-employed).
5. Tax settlement (skatteoppgjør) is usually sent between June and October.
6. If you owe tax, the due date is 20 August / 25 September.
7. Overpaid tax is refunded directly to your bank account.

b27f08b1-1b37-4852-9e9e-5d456db2d193.text:
## COMMON TAX QUESTIONS

Q: What is the deadline for filing my tax return?
A: 30 April for employees; 31 May for self-employed.

Q: How do I get a tax refund?
A: If the pre-filled return is correct and you are owed a refund, it is automatically
   paid after the tax settlement letter (skatteoppgjør) is issued (typically June–October).

Q: What documents do I need for a tax return as an immigrant?
A: D-number or national ID number (fødselsnummer), documentation of income from all sources
   (also from home country if applicable), bank interest certificates, and records of
   expenses you wish to deduct.

Q: Can I get a tax deduction for commuting?
A: Yes. If the distance between home and primary workplace exceeds 37 km per day,
   you can claim a commuting deduction of NOK 1.76 per km exceeding the threshold.

Q: As a new arrival, when am I liable for Norwegian tax?
A: You become a tax resident in Norway after staying 183 days in any 12-month period,
   or 270 days within a 36-month period.

a3a0440f-9e14-4ce5-b9a8-acb92f602469.text:
## NORWEGIAN CITIZENSHIP

### Requirements
- Must have lived in Norway for at least 7 of the last 10 years with a residence permit.
- Must pass a Norwegian language test at level A2 or higher.
- Must not have serious criminal convictions.
- Must renounce previous citizenship (with some exceptions).
- Children under 18 can acquire citizenship together with a parent.

ef837128-d326-4dd6-aa88-46a9e33330a4.text:
# Norwegian Immigration (UDI) – Domain Grounding Data
# Source: UDI – Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (www.udi.no) – for educational/demo use only

## OVERVIEW
UDI (Utlendingsdirektoratet) is the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration. It handles
applications for visas, residence permits, work permits, citizenship, and asylum.

42a13122-6647-4400-8251-c2fee36a953f.text:
## RESIDENCE PERMITS FOR NON-EEA CITIZENS

### Work Permit / Skilled Worker Permit
- Required before entry if you are a non-EEA/EFTA citizen who wants to work in Norway.
- You must have a concrete job offer from a Norwegian employer.
- The job must match the salary and working conditions of Norwegian collective agreements.
- Apply via Søknadsweb (udi.no/soknad) before arriving in Norway in most cases.

### Self-Employment Permit
- For non-EEA citizens who want to run their own business in Norway.
- Must demonstrate viable business plan and sufficient funding.

### Student Permit
- Must be enrolled in full-time study at an approved Norwegian institution.
- Applicants must document sufficient funds (approximately NOK 128,887/year, 2024).

### Family Immigration / Reunification
- Joining a spouse, cohabitant, or parent who is a Norwegian citizen or has a permanent
  residence permit.
- The reference person in Norway must meet income requirements
  (approximately NOK 321,751/year, 2024).

9a0ee63a-8612-4eed-ae2e-811734e5576d.text:
## WORK ASSESSMENT ALLOWANCE (AAP – ARBEIDSAVKLARINGSPENGER)

- Bridge between sick pay and work or disability benefit.
- Requires that your work capacity is reduced by at least 50% due to illness.
- 66% of previous income, minimum NOK 285,836 per year (2024).
- Maximum duration: typically 3 years (extendable in special circumstances).
